claim: The most cited Synology + GitHub Actions blog post deploys a single container to the NAS via Tailscale and a webhook — it does not branch on PR number, allocate per-PR URLs, or tear anything down on close.
quote: How to deploy an application from Github Actions to your Synology NAS — push docker containers built using GitHub Actions directly to your Synology without any human interaction, using Tailscale (built on top of WireGuard) and a webhook container.

claim: Damir's Corner only covers registering a self-hosted runner on the NAS, with no PR-preview, branch-deploy, or teardown logic anywhere in the post.
quote: This post does not cover per-PR preview deployments. It only explains how to set up a self-hosted GitHub Actions runner on a Synology NAS … no discussion of branch deployments, preview environments, or any automation related to pull requests beyond basic runner setup.

claim: The 2025 perspikapps Medium piece pitching the NAS as a 'CI/CD powerhouse' stops at builds, deploys to private infrastructure, and routine automation — preview-per-PR is not in scope.
quote: self-hosted GitHub Actions runners on Synology NAS devices for builds, deployments to private infrastructure, and routine automation … no mentions of preview deployments, PR-based deploys, or per-branch deployment URLs.

claim: PullPreview, the closest off-the-shelf 'review apps on your own infra' GitHub Action, supports exactly two providers and explicitly does not target a generic SSH host or NAS.
quote: PullPreview supports two providers: lightsail [and] hetzner … The page does not mention support for generic SSH servers, bring-your-own-VM solutions, self-hosted hardware, or services like Synology NAS.

claim: PullPreview's marketing reinforces this: it provisions VMs in your AWS/Hetzner account, not on hardware you already own — so users with a Synology can't drop it in.
quote: PullPreview supports Lightsail and Hetzner while keeping your code and infrastructure under your control … Lightsail AWS servers start at 3.5$/month for 512MB RAM, with the default instance type being 2GB at 10$/month.

claim: When developers ask publicly how to recreate Vercel-style PR previews on infrastructure they own, the answers default to vendor-managed offerings (Static Web Apps, deployment slots) — no self-hosted recipe is offered.
quote: automatic deployment of each pull request to a separate, isolated environment … unique URL for each PR (e.g., pr-123.ourapp.azurewebsites.net) … automatic cleanup when PRs are closed or merged … The answer does not mention any self-hosted or open-source solutions.

claim: Even purpose-built 'PR preview' projects on GitHub frame the gap explicitly — every team wants this, nobody wants to wire it up themselves — and ship as managed services rather than NAS-friendly recipes.
quote: every team wants preview environments but nobody wants to build them … Every pull request gets a complete production clone with frontend, backend, database seeded with test data, isolated network, custom subdomain, and SSL, and is torn down automatically on merge.

claim: The PullPreview author's own walk-through targets AWS, confirming there is no published 'PullPreview on a Synology' or equivalent end-to-end recipe in the indexable web.
quote: Automated preview deployments from GitHub to AWS in 10 minutes — once installed in your repository, this action is triggered whenever a change is made to pull requests labeled with pullpreview, continuously deploys updates from pull requests, and reports preview URLs in the GitHub UI.
